examples.title: Algorithm: Feedforward Neural Network
examples.description: Pseudocode for a basic feedforward neural network
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FeedforwardNeuralNetwork
INPUT: input vector x, weights matrix W, bias vector b
OUTPUT: output vector y

BEGIN
 FOR each layer i FROM 1 TO num_layers
 FOR each neuron j IN layer i
 compute weighted sum: z_j = W_ij * x_j + b_j
 apply activation function: y_j = sigmoid(z_j)
 END FOR
 END FOR
 RETURN output vector y
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates a basic feedforward neural network with multiple layers. It iterates over each layer and each neuron in the layer, computing the weighted sum and applying the activation function.

examples.title: Python: Convolutional Neural Network
examples.description: Python implementation of a convolutional neural network using Keras
examples.code:
from keras.models import Sequential
from keras.layers import Conv2D, MaxPooling2D, Flatten, Dense

# Define the model architecture
model = Sequential()
model.add(Conv2D(32, (3, 3), activation='relu', input_shape=(224, 224, 3)))
model.add(MaxPooling2D((2, 2)))
model.add(Conv2D(64, (3, 3), activation='relu'))
model.add(MaxPooling2D((2, 2)))
model.add(Conv2D(128, (3, 3), activation='relu'))
model.add(MaxPooling2D((2, 2)))
model.add(Flatten())
model.add(Dense(128, activation='relu'))
model.add(Dense(10, activation='softmax'))

# Compile the model
model.compile(optimizer='adam', loss='categorical_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])
examples.explanation: This Python code defines a convolutional neural network using the Keras library. It consists of multiple convolutional and pooling layers, followed by fully connected layers. The model is then compiled with the Adam optimizer and categorical cross-entropy loss.

examples.title: C: Recurrent Neural Network
examples.description: C implementation of a basic recurrent neural network
examples.explanation: This C code defines a basic recurrent neural network with multiple layers. It includes functions to compute the output of a neuron and to train the RNN.
